metadata: Weekly brief for week of 2026-05-25. RSS still fully blocked (22/22 denied, networkPolicyBlocked:true) — web search via WebSearch tool used for all intelligence. 7 new intelligence items reviewed and added. 3 new content drafts added (trifecta piece-to-camera, luxury brands post, NEM pre-panel piece-to-camera). Awards.json corrections: StreamTV Awards 2026 deadline corrected (finalists announced May 12, window closed — previous Jun 15 deadline was wrong); Campaign Asia 40 Under 40 deadline corrected to April 17 (was Jun 30, window closed); MARKETING-INTERACTIVE deadline corrected to June 12 (was June 5). settings.json weeklyFocus updated. Defining story this run: Netflix + Amazon + Disney trifecta — all three major US streamers now have vertical video infrastructure, confirmed week of May 4-10. New signals: Issa Rae 'Screen Time' 100M views on PineDrama; Marc Jacobs luxury microdrama; Park Min-Kyu (Single's Inferno) cast in FlareFlow; LA Vertical Drama Market second edition (May 7-10, Apollo Awards); 'Clearly me' AI consent violation (Category 22). 24 OPPORTUNITY_RADAR categories walked: 1-13, 17-24 produced signals. Categories still network-blocked: app-store chart rankings, Mandarin reputation watch (Weibo/Baidu 短剧 出海), Reddit qualitative (r/microdrama).

First real intelligence sweep. RSS still fully blocked (22/22 denied, networkPolicyBlocked:true) — web search via WebSearch tool used for all intelligence. 13 industry items reviewed and added to intelligence.json. 3 new content drafts added (Peacock reaction piece-to-camera, COL new hires post, NEM Dubrovnik vlog beat sheet). ATxEnterprise Singapore speaking event (May 20-22, done) added to speaking.json. Weekly brief for 2026-05-18 replaced (was low-value verification run). settings.json updated: Cannes Lions 2026 deadline flagged as passed (April 9). Key signal this run: Peacock licensed ReelShort content — first major US streamer to treat a microdrama-native platform as a formal content supplier. Three actionable opportunities this week: Peacock reaction piece-to-camera (Post, ~25 min), StreamTV Awards (Act now, ~90 min), pre-LA piece-to-camera (Post, ~25 min). 24 OPPORTUNITY_RADAR categories walked — categories 1-13, 17-23 produced signals; categories 14 (LinkedIn voices), 15 (public support), 16 (DM list beyond Anna Saalfeld), app-store chart intelligence, Mandarin reputation watch (Weibo/Baidu 短剧 出海), and Reddit qualitative remain network-blocked.

lowValueReason: Routine check on the new 'Hollywood for mobile phones' model (PR #7 — radar categories 21–24, on-camera voice rules, ≤3 actionable cap, time budget). Brief and content drafts refreshed: previous brief had 5 'Act on this week' items and 0 visual content drafts — both now corrected (3 actionable items each with time estimates, 2 of 4 drafts are now visual formats: piece-to-camera + travel vlog beat sheet). All 24 radar categories walked; gaps surfaced for app-store charts, Mandarin reputation watch, Reddit qualitative — all require network access. RSS sources still blocked (networkPolicyBlocked: true on last fetch, 25 of 26 sources host_not_allowed) so intel sweep was again skipped; environment-side allowlist fix is the only remaining unblocker.
metadata: Second routine-check run on the new Claude-Code-routine contract. Validates the PR #7 model changes — visual content mandatory, actionable cap at 3, time estimates on every actionable opportunity.

lowValueReason: Verification run only. npm run feeds reported networkPolicyBlocked: true (25 of 26 sources host_not_allowed) — the verification environment's outbound egress allowlist does not cover the RSS hostnames. Brief produced from existing seed state (awards, speaking, media, contacts, press) and the documented weekly focus. Code-level fix shipped this run: realistic User-Agent, denied/timeout/upstream error classification, networkPolicyBlocked flag, Google News RSS search fallbacks. The remaining unblocker is environment-level — the routine's Claude Code on the web environment policy needs the RSS hosts on its allowlist (see knowledge/OPPORTUNITY_RADAR.md → 'Environment network policy').
metadata: First successful routine run after the AI-agent-pipeline → Claude-Code-routine rebuild. Validates the new contract end-to-end.
